Enumeration Type Documentation

◆ breast_laterality

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::breast_laterality
strong

Breast laterality enumeration.

Specifies which breast is being imaged. This is a critical attribute for mammography workflow and helps prevent laterality errors in clinical practice.

See also
DICOM Tag (0020,0060) - Laterality
DICOM Tag (0020,0062) - Image Laterality
Enumerator
left 

Left breast (DICOM value: "L")

right 

Right breast (DICOM value: "R")

bilateral 

Both breasts (DICOM value: "B") - used for comparison views.

unknown 

Unknown or unspecified laterality.

Definition at line 105 of file mg_storage.hpp.

105 {
106 left,
107 right,
108 bilateral,
109 unknown
110};
kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::breast_laterality::right
@ right
Right breast (DICOM value: "R")
kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::breast_laterality::left
@ left
Left breast (DICOM value: "L")
kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::breast_laterality::bilateral
@ bilateral
Both breasts (DICOM value: "B") - used for comparison views.

◆ cad_processing_status

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::cad_processing_status
strong

CAD (Computer-Aided Detection) processing status.

Indicates whether and how CAD analysis has been performed on the image.

Enumerator
not_processed 

CAD has not been run on this image.

processed_no_findings 

CAD completed with no findings.

processed_findings 

CAD completed with findings.

processing_failed 

CAD processing failed.

pending 

CAD processing is pending.

Definition at line 350 of file mg_storage.hpp.

352 processed_no_findings,
353 processed_findings,
354 processing_failed,
355 pending
356};

@ not_processed
CAD has not been run on this image.

@ processed_no_findings
CAD completed with no findings.
kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::cad_processing_status::processing_failed
@ processing_failed
CAD processing failed.
kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::cad_processing_status::processed_findings
@ processed_findings
CAD completed with findings.

◆ dx_detector_type

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::dx_detector_type
strong

DX detector technology types.

Indicates the type of digital detector used for image acquisition.

Enumerator
direct 

Direct conversion (a-Se based)

indirect 

Indirect conversion (scintillator + photodiode)

storage 

Storage phosphor (CR-like)

film 

Film digitizer (rare for DX)

◆ dx_photometric_interpretation

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::dx_photometric_interpretation
strong

Supported photometric interpretations for DX images.

DX images are always grayscale. MONOCHROME1 means high values are dark (as on film), while MONOCHROME2 means high values are bright.

Enumerator
monochrome1 

Minimum pixel value = white (inverted)

monochrome2 

Minimum pixel value = black (standard)

Definition at line 130 of file dx_storage.hpp.

130 {
131 monochrome1,
132 monochrome2
133};

◆ dx_view_position

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::dx_view_position
strong

Common radiographic view positions for DX images.

View position indicates the direction of the X-ray beam relative to the patient and detector.

Enumerator
ap 

Anterior-Posterior.

pa 

Posterior-Anterior.

lateral 

Lateral (left or right)

oblique 

Oblique projection.

other 

Other/unspecified.

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::mg_image_type
strong

Mammography image purpose classification.

Mammography images can be either for presentation (display-ready with applied processing) or for processing (raw data requiring additional image processing).

Enumerator
for_presentation 

Ready for display and diagnosis.

for_processing 

Raw data requiring further processing.

◆ mg_view_position

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::mg_view_position
strong

Mammography-specific view positions.

Standard mammography views as defined by the ACR (American College of Radiology) and used in DICOM. View position is essential for proper image interpretation and comparison studies.

See also
DICOM Tag (0018,5101) - View Position
ACR BI-RADS Mammography Atlas
Enumerator
cc 

Craniocaudal - standard superior-inferior view.

mlo 

Mediolateral Oblique - angled lateral view (most common)

ml 

Mediolateral - true lateral (medial to lateral)

lm 

Lateromedial - true lateral (lateral to medial)

xccl 

Exaggerated CC Laterally - for lateral breast tissue.

xccm 

Exaggerated CC Medially - for medial breast tissue.

fb 

From Below - inferior to superior view.

sio 

Superolateral to Inferomedial Oblique.

iso 

Inferomedial to Superolateral Oblique.

cv 

Cleavage View - for medial breast tissue.

at 

Axillary Tail - for axillary extension.

spot 

Spot compression view.

mag 

Magnification view.

spot_mag 

Spot compression with magnification.

rl 

Rolled Lateral.

rm 

Rolled Medial.

rs 

Rolled Superior.

ri 

Rolled Inferior.

tangen 

Tangential view.

implant 

Implant displaced view (Eklund technique)

id 

Implant Displaced (alternate code)

other 

Other or unspecified view.

Definition at line 147 of file mg_storage.hpp.

147 {
148 // Standard screening views
149 cc,
150 mlo,
151
152 // Additional diagnostic views
153 ml,
154 lm,
155 xccl,
156 xccm,
157 fb,
158 sio,
159 iso,
160 cv,
161 at,
162
163 // Spot/magnification views
164 spot,
165 mag,
166 spot_mag,
167 rl,
168 rm,
169 rs,
170 ri,
171
172 // Specialized views
173 tangen,
174 implant,
175 id,
176
177 // Other
178 other
179};

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::pixel_value_representation
strong

Pixel value representation for parametric map data.

strong

US Region spatial format.

Defines the spatial organization of ultrasound regions as specified in DICOM PS3.3 Section C.8.5.5

none 

No geometric information.

2D format (sector, linear, etc.)

m_mode 

M-mode (time-motion)

spectral 

Spectral Doppler.

Physiological waveform.

Common XA frame rates in frames per second.

XA acquisitions typically use specific standardized frame rates for cardiac and vascular imaging.

Enumerator

7.5 fps (low dose)

fps_15 

15 fps (standard)

fps_30 

30 fps (high temporal resolution)

◆ xa_photometric_interpretation

enum class kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::xa_photometric_interpretation
strong

Supported photometric interpretations for XA/XRF images.

XA images are grayscale-only. MONOCHROME1 is traditional for XA (minimum pixel = white, like a lightbox view), while MONOCHROME2 is also supported.

Enumerator
monochrome1 

Minimum pixel = white (traditional X-ray view)

monochrome2 

Minimum pixel = black.

Definition at line 124 of file xa_storage.hpp.

124 {
125 monochrome1,
126 monochrome2
127};

strong

Positioner motion type.

No movement during acquisition.

dynamic 

Positioner moves during acquisition (e.g., rotational)

std::string kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::create_mg_image_type ( bool  is_original,
bool  is_primary,
mg_image_type  type 
)

Create DICOM-compliant image type value for mammography.

Constructs the Image Type (0008,0008) value for mammography images according to DICOM specifications.

Parameters
is_originaltrue if ORIGINAL, false if DERIVED
is_primarytrue if PRIMARY, false if SECONDARY
typeFor Presentation or For Processing
Returns
Properly formatted Image Type value

Definition at line 408 of file mg_storage.cpp.

411 {
412
413 std::string result;
414
415 // Value 1: ORIGINAL or DERIVED
416 result += is_original ? "ORIGINAL" : "DERIVED";
417 result += "\\";
418
419 // Value 2: PRIMARY or SECONDARY
420 result += is_primary ? "PRIMARY" : "SECONDARY";
421 result += "\\";
422
423 // Value 3: Image flavor - empty for mammography typically
424 result += "\\";
425
426 // Value 4: Derived Pixel Contrast - specific for For Presentation/Processing
427 if (type == mg_image_type::for_presentation) {
428 result += "NONE"; // or specific processing type
429 } else {
430 result += ""; // empty for processing images
431 }
432
433 return result;
434}

286 result.emplace_back(info.uid);
287 }
288 // Include mammography if requested
289 else if (info.is_mammography && include_mammography) {
290 result.emplace_back(info.uid);
291 }
292 // Include intra-oral if requested
293 else if (info.is_intraoral && include_intraoral) {
294 result.emplace_back(info.uid);
295 }
296 }
297

299}

Get recommended transfer syntaxes for DX images.

48 // Explicit VR Little Endian (preferred for interoperability)

51 "1.2.840.10008.1.2",
52 // HTJ2K Lossless (high-throughput lossless, preserves quantitative data)
53 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.201",
54 // JPEG Lossless (for diagnostic quality - recommended for quantitative data)
55 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.70",
56 // JPEG 2000 Lossless (better compression, preserves quantitative values)
57 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.90",
58 // RLE Lossless
59 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.5"
60 };
61}

280 {
281 switch (isotope) {
282 case nm_radioisotope::tc99m:
283 return 140.0;
284 case nm_radioisotope::i131:
285 return 364.0;
286 case nm_radioisotope::i123:
287 return 159.0;
288 case nm_radioisotope::tl201:
289 return 71.0; // Primary peak (also 167 keV)
290 case nm_radioisotope::ga67:
291 return 93.0; // Primary peak (also 185, 300 keV)
292 case nm_radioisotope::in111:
293 return 171.0; // Primary peak (also 245 keV)
294 case nm_radioisotope::f18:
295 return 511.0; // Annihilation photons
296 case nm_radioisotope::other:
297 return 0.0;
298 }
299 return 0.0;

◆ get_xa_transfer_syntaxes()

std::vector< std::string > kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::get_xa_transfer_syntaxes ( )

Get recommended transfer syntaxes for XA images.

Returns a prioritized list of transfer syntaxes suitable for X-Ray angiographic image storage. XA images are typically grayscale with high bit depth (8-16 bits).

Returns
Vector of transfer syntax UIDs in priority order

Definition at line 47 of file xa_storage.cpp.

47 {
48 return {
49 // Explicit VR Little Endian (preferred for interoperability)
50 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.1",

52 "1.2.840.10008.1.2",
53 // HTJ2K Lossless (high-throughput lossless for multi-frame)
54 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.201",
55 // JPEG Lossless (for diagnostic quality)
56 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.4.70",
57 // RLE Lossless (for multi-frame)
58 "1.2.840.10008.1.2.5"

mg_view_position  view 
)
noexcept

Check if laterality and view position are consistent.

Validates that the laterality and view position make clinical sense (e.g., bilateral laterality shouldn't be used with single-breast views).

Parameters
lateralityThe breast laterality
viewThe view position
Returns
true if the combination is clinically valid

Definition at line 373 of file mg_storage.cpp.

375 {
376
377 // Bilateral laterality should only be used for comparison views
378 // or special study types, not for standard single-breast views
379 if (laterality == breast_laterality::bilateral) {
380 // CV (cleavage view) can legitimately be bilateral
381 if (view == mg_view_position::cv) {
382 return true;
383 }
384 // Most standard views should specify L or R
385 return false;
386 }
387
388 // Unknown laterality with a specific view is problematic
389 if (laterality == breast_laterality::unknown) {
390 // Allow if view is also unspecified
391 return view == mg_view_position::other;
392 }
393
394 // Left or Right with any view is valid
395 return true;
396}

References bilateral, cv, other, and unknown.

◆ parse_breast_laterality()

breast_laterality kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::parse_breast_laterality ( std::string_view  value)
noexcept

Parse DICOM laterality string to enum.

Parameters
valueThe DICOM string value
Returns
The corresponding enum value, or unknown if not recognized

Definition at line 57 of file mg_storage.cpp.

57 {
58 if (value.empty()) {
59 return breast_laterality::unknown;
60 }
61
62 // Handle single character codes
63 if (value.size() == 1) {
64 switch (std::toupper(static_cast<unsigned char>(value[0]))) {
65 case 'L': return breast_laterality::left;
66 case 'R': return breast_laterality::right;
67 case 'B': return breast_laterality::bilateral;
68 }
69 }
70
71 // Handle full words (case-insensitive)
72 std::string lower_value;
73 lower_value.reserve(value.size());
74 for (char c : value) {
75 lower_value += static_cast<char>(std::tolower(static_cast<unsigned char>(c)));
76 }
77
78 if (lower_value == "left") return breast_laterality::left;
79 if (lower_value == "right") return breast_laterality::right;
80 if (lower_value == "bilateral" || lower_value == "both") {
81 return breast_laterality::bilateral;
82 }
83
84 return breast_laterality::unknown;
85}

References bilateral, left, right, and unknown.

159 {

83 }
84 // Default to MONOCHROME2 for DX
85 return dx_photometric_interpretation::monochrome2;
86}

◆ parse_mg_view_position()

mg_view_position kcenon::pacs::services::sop_classes::parse_mg_view_position ( std::string_view  value)
noexcept

Parse DICOM view position string to mammography view enum.

Parameters
valueThe DICOM string value
Returns
The corresponding enum value, or other if not recognized

Definition at line 123 of file mg_storage.cpp.

123 {
124 if (value.empty()) {
125 return mg_view_position::other;
126 }
127
128 // Convert to uppercase for comparison
129 std::string upper_value;
130 upper_value.reserve(value.size());
131 for (char c : value) {
132 upper_value += static_cast<char>(std::toupper(static_cast<unsigned char>(c)));
133 }
134
135 // Standard views
136 if (upper_value == "CC") return mg_view_position::cc;
137 if (upper_value == "MLO") return mg_view_position::mlo;
138 if (upper_value == "ML") return mg_view_position::ml;
139 if (upper_value == "LM") return mg_view_position::lm;
140
141 // Extended CC views
142 if (upper_value == "XCCL") return mg_view_position::xccl;
143 if (upper_value == "XCCM") return mg_view_position::xccm;
144
145 // Other standard views
146 if (upper_value == "FB") return mg_view_position::fb;
147 if (upper_value == "SIO") return mg_view_position::sio;
148 if (upper_value == "ISO") return mg_view_position::iso;
149 if (upper_value == "CV") return mg_view_position::cv;
150 if (upper_value == "AT") return mg_view_position::at;
151
152 // Spot/magnification views
153 if (upper_value == "SPOT") return mg_view_position::spot;
154 if (upper_value == "MAG") return mg_view_position::mag;
155 if (upper_value == "SPOT MAG" || upper_value == "SPOTMAG") {
156 return mg_view_position::spot_mag;
157 }
158
159 // Rolled views
160 if (upper_value == "RL") return mg_view_position::rl;
161 if (upper_value == "RM") return mg_view_position::rm;
162 if (upper_value == "RS") return mg_view_position::rs;
163 if (upper_value == "RI") return mg_view_position::ri;
164
165 // Specialized views
166 if (upper_value == "TAN" || upper_value == "TANGENTIAL") {
167 return mg_view_position::tangen;
168 }
169 if (upper_value == "ID" || upper_value == "IMPLANT DISPLACED" ||
170 upper_value == "IMPLANTDISPLACED") {
171 return mg_view_position::implant;
172 }
173
174 return mg_view_position::other;
175}

References at, cc, cv, fb, implant, iso, lm, mag, ml, mlo, other, ri, rl, rm, rs, sio, spot, spot_mag, tangen, xccl, and xccm.

211 if (value == "INFERRED FROM") return sr_relationship_type::inferred_from;
212 if (value == "SELECTED FROM") return sr_relationship_type::selected_from;
213 return sr_relationship_type::unknown;
214}
